summary: News articles published within the previous 24 hours have outlined several specific developments in small UAS operations. Ukraine has achieved a world record with the first-ever FPV drone strike on a Russian warship at over 350 kilometers, and has also tested FPV drones targeting anti-drone electronic warfare systems. Reports indicate that Russia is flooding the battlefield with fiber optic drones, prompting Ukraine to race to develop countermeasures, while unjammable drones are leaving wires everywhere, forcing Ukrainian troops to move with caution. Additionally, Ukrainian forces have destroyed Russia’s state-of-the-art Malva self-propelled gun using an FPV drone, and the conflict is giving rise to a new kind of drone warfare involving hit-to-kill intercepts.
